title: pkg,libpod: remove `pkg/hooks` and use `hooks` from `c/common`
body:
PR https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1071 moved `pkg/hooks` to
`c/common` hence remove that from podman and use `pkg/hooks` from
`c/common`


[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]
[NO TESTS NEEDED]

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
pkg: hooks has been removed from podman to github.com/containers/common ( https://github.com/containers/common/tree/main/pkg/hooks )
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *flouthoc* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Is failing test related to this PR.
`install: missing destination file operand after 'podman_tmp_wXKL/podman-v4.2.0-dev/usr/share/man/man5'`

> Is failing test related to this PR. `install: missing destination file operand after 'podman_tmp_wXKL/podman-v4.2.0-dev/usr/share/man/man5'`

Yes you need to update the makefile with the removed man page

@Luap99 Did you mean https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/Makefile ? There was only a reference to `pkg/hooks` in `podman.spec.rpkg` I have removed it already can't see any reference in Makefile :\

```diff
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 32de58856..ef91b5071 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -77,7 +77,7 @@ BUILDTAGS_CROSS ?= containers_image_openpgp exclude_graphdriver_btrfs exclude_gr
 CONTAINER_RUNTIME := $(shell command -v podman 2> /dev/null || echo docker)
 OCI_RUNTIME ?= ""
 
-MANPAGES_MD ?= $(wildcard docs/source/markdown/*.md pkg/*/docs/*.md)
+MANPAGES_MD ?= $(wildcard docs/source/markdown/*.md)
 MANPAGES ?= $(MANPAGES_MD:%.md=%)
 MANPAGES_DEST ?= $(subst markdown,man, $(subst source,build,$(MANPAGES)))
 
```

@Luap99 wouldn't it stop processing `docs` from other packages as well since its `pkg/*/docs/*.md` ? 

Edit: I don't see any other packages containing docs so i guess we can add it later again if something shows up
@Luap99 Nope patch did not work looks like something else. Trying to check..

Looks like you have to remove the .5 install comand as well since there are no longer any .5 pages in the podman repo.
```diff
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 32de58856..f15b61efe 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -776,9 +776,7 @@ install.modules-load: # This should only be used by distros which might use ipta
 .PHONY: install.man
 install.man:
        install ${SELINUXOPT} -d -m 755 $(DESTDIR)$(MANDIR)/man1
-       install ${SELINUXOPT} -d -m 755 $(DESTDIR)$(MANDIR)/man5
        install ${SELINUXOPT} -m 644 $(filter %.1,$(MANPAGES_DEST)) $(DESTDIR)$(MANDIR)/man1
-       install ${SELINUXOPT} -m 644 $(filter %.5,$(MANPAGES_DEST)) $(DESTDIR)$(MANDIR)/man5
        install ${SELINUXOPT} -m 644 docs/source/markdown/links/*1 $(DESTDIR)$(MANDIR)/man1
 
 .PHONY: install.completions
```

Weird it looks like old buildah and c/common are not happy together, bumping buildah as well since new `c/common` passes test with buildah on main.

Edit: verified locked buildah version `e66309ebde8c` in podman is not working with latest `c/common` so will need a buildah bump for this.

> Weird it looks like old buildah and c/common are not happy together, bumping buildah as well since new `c/common` passes test with buildah on main.
> 
> Edit: verified locked buildah version `e66309ebde8c` in podman is not working with latest `c/common` so will need a buildah bump for this.

Will rebase this when podman gets new buildah.
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL. Would it be possible to get this in before the release ?
@flouthoc +1 on backporting it to the v4.2 branch - assuming the c/common there ships these man pages.
@flouthoc please update the release for downstream packagers.  I'd expect an explicit note where these man pages should be built from now.

> @flouthoc please update the release for downstream packagers. I'd expect an explicit note where these man pages should be built from now.

@vrothberg I added a release note on the PR for this. Is there some place else other than this where we have to update this ? Could you please let me know if there is any other place as well so I can update that.
